violations.text: Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (sauces) in the prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

The door to the outside, located at the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
Live cat observed within facility. Live animals may not be allowed on the premises of a food establishment. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
Freezer door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
Glass located on steam table was not in good repair, observed cracked glass. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. 1. Steam table 2. Fryers 3. Flat top grill 4. Stove 5. Refrigeration shelving 6. Fans (Walk-in cooler) 7. Grease trap 8. Hoods New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

At time of inspection, plumbing in basement was in poor repair. Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. Plumber was contacted prior to inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

At time of inspection, grey water was observed in basement. Per interview with PIC, plumping had a leak. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The floors and walls in the walk in cooler area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the outside dumpster area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2023
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods cheese in the prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

Various cheeses and sliced tomatoes was observed held at 46-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.Disposed Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

Various meats, stews and rice was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

The presence of rodents and roaches is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings(under serving line shelves) and roaches on glue traps, and shelving. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the: 1.Shelves under hot hold unit 2.Shelves in retail area 3.Food and beverage shelves in the walk in cooler 4.Shelves under the stove have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, roach carcass and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

The floors and walls in the walk in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the outside dumpster area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023
